$schema_note: SHARED cross-department SOP manifest for the Email Engine (Skill 50). This is the universal-sops face of the capability: it is how ANY department (marketing / crm / sales / web-development / social-media / personal-assistant) discovers and drives the end-to-end email pipeline. It does NOT re-implement the engine. The authoritative machine spine lives in the skill: 50-email-engine/EMAIL-MANIFEST.json (phase order + AF codes), 50-email-engine/tools/prove-email.py (the fail-closed floor prover), 50-email-engine/run_email_engine.py (the deterministic orchestrator, front-door-nonce gated by email-engine-entry.sh), and the Email Superlibrary 50-email-engine/email-library/ (13 frameworks + 4 buyer-types + 4 objectives + 12 persona-styles + 3 sequences) selected by 50-email-engine/tools/email_matcher_cli.py. This file mirrors the fb-ad-craft / presentation-slide-craft enforcement-spine pattern so a department reads ONE manifest to know the phases, the owning roles, the SOP for each phase, and the gate codes. SOP-LOCKED: any added/changed SOP, framework rule, or gate MUST stay in lockstep with 50-email-engine/EMAIL-MANIFEST.json and prove-email.py.
design_law: Enforcement, not description. The word/subject/CTA/format bands are SACRED (SOURCE-EMAIL-CORPUS.md): 150-300 words per email EXCEPT the 3-B Plan (< 150); exactly 2 A/B subject lines; preview count is sequence-declared (Convert&Flow master = 1, high-ticket = 2); the founder's real name signs every email (never a placeholder); a persona style is adopted for TONE ONLY and the person is NEVER named or quoted. A logged client-exact override wins over a default band and is recorded on the process certificate. Client runtime uses the CLIENT's OWN provider chain — NEVER Anthropic / claude-* ids or operator keys. Every deterministic gate (email_matcher.py, prove-email.py) is provider-neutral Python that runs identically on every box.

phases.name: Intake — lock the brief in ONE block (objective, buyer type, offer, brand voice, sequence position, founder name, high-ticket?)
phases.gate: prove-email.py --kind intake: skill==email-engine, asked_all_at_once:true, one_question_per_turn:false, all six required brief fields present. A self-attested completeness flag is NEVER trusted.

phases.name: Select — route the brief to a framework + buyer-type modifiers + objective tactics + (high-ticket) persona style + sequence
phases.gate: email_matcher_cli.py --match resolves to a canonical id; explicit user spec is always honored (flex mode); the match rationale + runners-up are logged.

phases.name: Generate — author corpus-faithful copy against the selected structure, in brand voice, at the correct sequence position
phases.gate: every slot carries subjects[2] + previews + body + framework + objective + ctas[]; a supplied sections[] matches the framework's declared part count (PASTOR = 6, Million Dollar Sales = 12).

phases.name: QC — fail-closed floor prover over the authored copy
phases.gate: prove-email.py PASS (exit 0) over working/copy/emails.json. ANY AF-EMAIL-* violation blocks advance to P4; the failing email re-authors ONLY the failing item (bounded retry). A measurer, not an agent self-score.

phases.name: Deploy — DRAFT-ONLY Skill-44 build plan (workflow email steps); nothing sends
phases.gate: a signed process certificate is required (signed:true + signed_by) before deploy; the emitted GHL workflow is a DRAFT built by caf (44-convert-and-flow-operator); a hand-rolled sender in the run dir is refused. Skill 44's qc-built-workflow.sh >= 8.5 runs on the deployed draft.

phases.name: Approve — the ONE human pause: a person approves before any send; the process certificate is issued on a full P1->P4 pass
phases.gate: approval.json approved:true + approved_by; run_email_engine.py issues the delivery PROCESS-CERTIFICATE only after a full P1->P4 pass (no phase skips, all_phases_pass:true). The certificate attests the governed pipeline ran in order; it never authorizes a send.

owning_roles.note: Existing role-library seats gain the Email Engine as a Section-8 tool; no new department is required. See 23-ai-workforce-blueprint/templates/role-library/.
